question: What evidence supports preemptive TEVAR for uncomplicated acute or subacute type B aortic dissection?
source_access_type: Original publisher PDF held in private local source workspace
source_version_or_date: Eur J Vasc Endovasc Surg. 2014;48:285-291; appended author-name correction present in PDF

study_characteristics.design: Prospective multicenter randomized controlled trial; intention-to-treat primary efficacy analysis
study_characteristics.population: 61 patients with acute uncomplicated type B aortic dissection less than 14 days from symptom onset
study_characteristics.intervention: Best medical treatment plus Gore TAG (n=30 randomized)
study_characteristics.primary_endpoint: Composite of incomplete/no false-lumen thrombosis, aortic dilatation, or aortic rupture at one year
study_characteristics.crossovers: Three early crossovers from BMT to TAG plus BMT due to progression

source_identity_validation.metadata_warning: The supplied publisher PDF appends a correction to a trialist name. The registered original article itself is present and matches the core identity fields; the correction is not a substitute article.

extracted_claims.claim_text: ADSORB randomized 61 patients with acute uncomplicated type B aortic dissection to best medical treatment with or without Gore TAG.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: Of the 61 patients randomised, 80% were DeBakey type IIIB
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Methods on printed pp. 286-287 specify onset under 14 days and allocation 31 versus 30.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for extraction; not specialist reviewed

extracted_claims.claim_text: At one year, the primary composite endpoint occurred less often with TAG plus medical treatment than with medical treatment alone.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: 15 (50.0%) of the 30 TAG+BMT patients had at least one endpoint event
extracted_claims.source_location_note: All 31 BMT patients met the composite; P<0.001. Missing imaging was counted as failure.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for extraction; not specialist reviewed

extracted_claims.claim_text: Incomplete false-lumen thrombosis at one year was less frequent with TAG plus medical treatment.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: Incomplete false lumen thrombosis was found in 13 (43%)
extracted_claims.confidence: High for extraction; not specialist reviewed

extracted_claims.claim_text: One-year imaging showed smaller false-lumen and larger true-lumen diameters with TAG plus medical treatment.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: maximum false lumen diameter was 18.5 mm for TAG+BMT versus 25.1 mm for BMT
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Maximum true-lumen diameter was 32.2 versus 25.5 mm; both P<0.001.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for extraction; not specialist reviewed

extracted_claims.claim_text: No aortic ruptures were reported in either randomized group within one year.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: No aortic ruptures were reported within 365 days
extracted_claims.confidence: High for extraction; not specialist reviewed

extracted_claims.claim_text: ADSORB was not powered to determine a mortality benefit, and the authors called for a larger randomized trial with longer follow-up.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: the ADSORB trial was not powered for mortality
extracted_claims.source_location_note: The preceding paragraph states survival can only be demonstrated with a larger RCT and longer follow-up.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for extraction; not specialist reviewed

outcome_data.population: Intention-to-treat efficacy population; missing imaging counted as failure

outcome_data.page_or_location: Printed p. 288; PDF p. 4; Dissection-related mortality

limitations: The trial was not powered for mortality.
Fourteen efficacy failures reflected inadequate imaging or dropout, and missing imaging was counted as failure.
Three patients crossed from BMT to TAG plus BMT within five days because of progression.
Experienced tertiary-center recruitment may introduce selection bias and limit generalizability.
No complete screening log was maintained; more than half of logged candidates were excluded, mainly for anatomical limitations.
The trial was funded by W. L. Gore, and three authors reported consulting relationships with Gore.
contradictions: The trial demonstrated imaging/remodeling differences but did not establish a survival benefit.
reviewer_notes: Original-PDF extraction only. The appended name-correction notice was noted but was not used as a substitute source. No claim or outcome is citation verified or specialist validated.
